sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Kenneth Query Sells Shares of Nucor Corp (NUE)
feed.summary: Kenneth Query, Executive Vice President of a Nucor Corp subsidiary, sold 7,452 shares of NUE on February 11, 2026. This transaction continues a pattern of insider selling, with 17 insider sells and no buys recorded over the past year. Nucor Corp's stock is currently trading at $196.02, making it modestly overvalued according to GuruFocus's GF Value.

feed.title: Chipotle to open 3rd location in League City
feed.summary: Chipotle is opening its third location in League City, Texas, in mid-February 2026. The restaurant will be located at 1603 W. League City Parkway and will offer customizable Mexican-inspired food like burritos, bowls, and salads, with an official opening date of February 17. Catherine White of Community Impact reported on this development.

feed.title: How Chipotle lost its sizzle
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill experienced its worst year ever, with same-store sales declining for the first time since its IPO two decades ago. This downturn is attributed to a broader slowdown in fast-casual chains, as consumers, especially those with middle and lower incomes, are cutting back on discretionary spending and seeking better value due to economic anxieties. While Chipotle plans to cater to its higher-income customers, analysts suggest it needs to broaden its appeal to more cost-conscious consumers, much like how McDonald's saw a sales surge with its $5 meal deal.

feed.title: Baltimore public company lays off 54 employees
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price, a Baltimore-based public company, has laid off 54 employees at its headquarters. This action follows a nearly 5% reduction in the firm's global headcount last year, even as its revenue increased and profits remained stable.

feed.title: Arista Earnings: AI Keeps the Beats and Raises Coming and Gives Investors Opportunity
feed.summary: Arista Networks' Q4 results exceeded expectations, leading to an increased 2026 sales outlook to $11.25 billion and a higher AI revenue guidance of $3.25 billion. The company is benefiting from strong AI infrastructure demand and holds a leadership position in AI Ethernet. Morningstar has raised its fair value estimate for Arista to $175 per share, citing continued stellar growth driven by AI spending.

feed.title: PRICE T ROWE GROUP INC SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price Group Inc. released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing a 3.1% increase in net revenues to $7,314.8 million but a 6.2% decrease in operating income due to restructuring charges. The company focused on aligning expense growth with revenue, expanding its global footprint, and enhancing client relationships. Key strategic initiatives included targeted role eliminations, technology outsourcing, increased dividends, and share repurchases, while addressing market, operational, regulatory, and human capital risks.

feed.title: CME Cash Market Summary
feed.summary: The article provides the CME Cash Market Summary as of February 13, 2026, including spot dairy market prices. It also features a "Dairy Perspective" morning commentary discussing inflation data, stock futures, the dollar index, Treasury yields, crude oil prices, and the grain and oilseed markets. The content is presented by StoneX, a financial services company offering various market insights and solutions.

feed.title: Forecasting The Future: 13 Analyst Projections For American International Gr
feed.summary: Thirteen analysts have provided their projections for American International Group (NYSE: AIG) over the last three months, showing a range of perspectives with an average 12-month price target of $87.46, a 1.12% decrease from the previous average. While the company boasts a high market capitalization, it faces challenges in revenue growth, net margin, return on equity, and return on assets. However, AIG's debt management is strong, with a debt-to-equity ratio below the industry average.

feed.title: A Closer Look at Qualcomm's Options Market Dynamics
feed.summary: Deep-pocketed investors have shown a significant interest in Qualcomm (NASDAQ: QCOM) through the options market, with 39 unusual options activities detected. There's a divided sentiment, with 43% bullish and 30% bearish, and major market movers are targeting a price range of $100.0 to $190.0 for the stock. Qualcomm's stock is currently trading at $141.12, up 1.91%, with its next earnings report due in 75 days, and analysts have provided an average target price of $149.8.

feed.title: PNC Financial's Arm Rolls Out Premier Client for Mass-Affluent Clients
feed.summary: PNC Financial Services Group, Inc. has launched PNC Premier Client, a new integrated banking and investment experience aimed at emerging and mass-affluent individuals and families with over $100,000 in assets. This offering combines banking, lending, insurance, and investment planning with dedicated support from personal bankers and financial advisors. The initiative mirrors a broader industry trend among large U.S. banks to cater to affluent clients through integrated service models.

feed.title: For Second Straight Year, Drake State Takes Part In FICO Educational Analytics Challenge
feed.summary: Drake State Community & Technical College is participating in the FICO Educational Analytics Challenge for the second consecutive year. This program, focusing on AI and large language models for financial applications, aims to increase diversity in data science by mentoring students from HBCUs like Drake State. The challenge provides hands-on experience with AI development and financial technology, preparing students for careers in the field.

feed.title: Travel retail shines for Philip Morris International as smoke-free drive accelerates
feed.summary: Philip Morris International (PMI) reported a strong financial performance in 2025, with net revenues up +7.3% to US$40.6 billion, largely driven by its smoke-free business which now represents 41.5% of revenues. Travel retail emerged as a top-performing channel for PMI's smoke-free products, particularly IQOS, with airports showing the highest year-on-year growth in consumables. This highlights the strategic importance of travel retail in PMI's global smoke-free transformation efforts, aiming to expand access to science-backed smoke-free alternatives for adult nicotine users.

feed.title: 3 Undervalued Technology Hardware, Storage & Peripherals Stocks for Friday, February 13
feed.summary: This article identifies three undervalued technology hardware, storage, and peripherals stocks for February 13, 2026. The featured tickers are CRSR, HPE, and HPQ, suggesting opportunities for investors based on their valuation. The content implies that this analysis is part of a larger investment research offering for subscribers.

feed.title: Ponce Financial EVP Kouzilos sells $183k in shares By Investing.com
feed.summary: Ponce Financial Group's EVP Ioannis Kouzilos sold 10,929 shares for $183,716 but also acquired 11,929 shares through options exercises totaling $125,518 on February 11, 2026. Separately, Marlene Cintron, with extensive public policy and economic development experience, was appointed to Ponce Bank's Board of Directors to strengthen its leadership.

feed.title: Magna earnings, shares jump as better margins overshadow tariffs
feed.summary: Magna's revenue rose in the fourth quarter despite a decline in global vehicle production, leading to a jump in earnings and share prices. Improved margins helped the company overcome the impact of tariffs. This positive performance is reflected in higher 2025 earnings forecasts.

feed.title: Nokia claims network CTO from Juniper Networks
feed.summary: Pavan Kurapati, formerly VP for technical solutions architecture at Juniper Networks, has joined Nokia as SVP and CTO for data center networking. This move follows several other high-profile departures from Juniper post-HPE acquisition, and Kurapati's new role focuses on Nokia's technology strategy for AI data center networking. His background includes significant work on HPC/AI clusters, 5G infrastructure, and the Juniper Cloud Native Router.

feed.title: These 3 Zacks 'Strong Buy' Stocks Reflect the Hottest S&P 500 Stocks of 2026
feed.summary: This article highlights three Zacks #1 (Strong Buy) rated stocks—SanDisk (SNDK), Western Digital (WDC), and Teradyne (TER)—that are performing exceptionally well in 2026, largely due to the surging demand fueled by artificial intelligence. SanDisk and Western Digital benefit from increased storage needs for AI data centers, while Teradyne profits from the complex testing requirements of AI chips. These companies demonstrate significant revenue growth and strong stock performance, contrasting with other potentially struggling segments of the technology sector.

feed.title: Prologis Rewards Its Investors With a 5.9% Dividend Increase
feed.summary: Prologis, Inc. (PLD) recently announced a 5.9% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $1.07 per share, an annualized amount of $4.28, reflecting a 3.14% dividend yield based on its current share price. This marks the fifth dividend hike in five years for the industrial REIT, demonstrating a commitment to shareholder wealth. The company's strong operational performance, strategic property locations, and healthy balance sheet suggest the sustainability of this increased dividend.

feed.title: Maxeon Defeats Investor Lawsuit Alleging Veiled Cash Flow Crisis
feed.summary: Solar panel maker Maxeon Solar Technologies has successfully defended itself against an investor lawsuit for the second time. The suit alleged the company concealed a deteriorating cash flow situation and made false or misleading statements before a significant stock drop in 2024. A judge ruled that Maxeon's statements were more modest, claiming only that the company anticipated operating as a going concern for another 12 months, and the investor failed to sufficiently prove that the company hid a critical cash flow problem.

feed.title: XPO Inc (XPO) Trading Down 6.04% on Feb 12
feed.summary: Shares of XPO Inc (XPO) were down 6.04% on February 12, trading at $189.63. The stock is 8.41% below its 52-week high but 122.94% above its 52-week low. Wall Street analysts have an average target price of $193.63, suggesting a 2.11% upside, while GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $122.75, indicating a potential 35.27% downside.

feed.title: Xcel Energy Inc (XEL) Shares Up 3.51% on Feb 13
feed.summary: Shares of Xcel Energy Inc (XEL) rose 3.51% on February 13th, closing at $81.75. This places the stock just below its 52-week high, with Wall Street analysts forecasting an average price target of $87.69. However, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside based on its estimated GF Value.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Names John Chidsey As New CEO
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. announced John W. Chidsey as its new President and CEO, replacing Harry Sommer, which resulted in a stock decline due to investor uncertainty. Despite the leadership change, the company anticipates its Q4 2025 net yield and full-year 2025 results to align with prior guidance. The stock is currently showing short-term weakness and mixed momentum but has an analyst consensus of "Buy" with an average price target of $27.41.

feed.title: eBay Climate Plan Promotes Recommerce, Outlines Structural Shifts
feed.summary: eBay has released its first Climate Transition Plan, focusing on cutting emissions, promoting recommerce, and realigning governance to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2045. The plan emphasizes cultivating a circular marketplace, integrating climate action into company operations and governance, and collaborating across its value chain to reduce Scope 3 impacts. This initiative reflects eBay's commitment to sustainability and adapting to rising consumer demand for sustainable shopping practices.

feed.title: Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) Management Changes
feed.summary: Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) saw its stock rise slightly despite forecasting 2026 core funds from operations (FFO) below Wall Street estimates, indicating a potential softening in demand for self-storage. The company also announced that CEO Joe Russell will step down in March, to be succeeded by CFO Tom Boyle, with Joe Fisher taking over as the new CFO. Public Storage reported strong Q4 FY25 adjusted FFO and revenue, beating analysts' estimates, and detailed significant acquisitions and development projects.

feed.title: Power utility CMS Energy to pay $0.2625 on preferred shares Apr. 15
feed.summary: CMS Energy (NYSE: CMS) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.2625 per depositary share for its 4.200% Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series C. The dividend is payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2026. This dividend applies specifically to the Series C preferred shares (NYSE: CMS PRC) and not to common shareholders.

feed.title: Halper Sadeh LLC Encourages Five Below, Inc. Shareholders To Contact the Firm To Discuss Their Rights
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating whether officers and directors of Five Below, Inc. (NASDAQ: FIVE) breached their fiduciary duties to shareholders. The firm encourages long-term shareholders to contact them to discuss legal rights and options, which may include seeking corporate governance reforms or the return of funds. Shareholder participation can lead to improved company policies and enhanced shareholder value.

feed.title: International Paper Updates Executive Equity Awards and Details 2026 PSU Grants
feed.summary: International Paper (IP) has updated its 2026 Long-Term Incentive Plan (LTIP) disclosures, including additional performance stock units (PSUs) for Joseph R. Saab and new PSU grants for other senior executives. The company also detailed a one-time RSU award for its new Chief Human Resources Officer and the full vesting of a previous RSU award, emphasizing its strategy of performance-linked equity tied to total shareholder return and executive retention across its packaging and cellulose fibers operations.

feed.title: BIIB: Late-stage lupus therapies and a diverse immunology pipeline target major unmet needs
feed.summary: Biogen (BIIB) presented a strong immunology pipeline at a recent symposium, featuring promising late-stage lupus therapies with new mechanisms and trial designs. The company is focusing on unmet needs in SLE and CLE, while also developing early-stage programs in neuroinflammation and MS, indicating a multi-indication strategy for future growth. This information is based on an AI-generated summary of Biogen's Piper Sandler Virtual Novel Targets in Immunology Symposium Audio Transcript.

feed.title: Commit To Buy Keysight Technologies At $195, Earn 6.4% Annualized Using Options
feed.summary: Investors considering buying Keysight Technologies (KEYS) stock may benefit from using an options strategy, specifically selling puts at the $195 strike price. This strategy offers an annualized return of 6.4% by collecting a premium, with the investor committing to buy shares at $195 only if the price falls significantly. While it limits upside compared to owning shares, it provides an income stream and a potential entry point at a lower cost basis.

feed.title: What Fueled KEY's NII Expansion in 2025 Despite Easing Rates
feed.summary: KeyCorp (KEY) saw its net interest income (NII) rise by 23% in 2025 to $4.67 billion, driven by robust commercial loan growth, improved deposit trends, and lower funding costs, even with Federal Reserve rate cuts. Peers PNC Financial Services Group and Huntington Bancshares also experienced NII growth. KeyCorp anticipates NII to increase by 8-10% in 2026, supported by expected net interest margin expansion and loan growth.

feed.title: Harley-Davidson is pulling back on motorcycle production in SHOCK announcement
feed.summary: Harley-Davidson announced a major shift in strategy, scaling back motorcycle production to align inventory with customer demand. New CEO Artie Starrs revealed the company will instead focus on expanding its parts and accessories business, offering more customization options for riders. This move comes after a tumultuous year for the company, including the ousting of its former CEO and the closure of several dealerships.

feed.title: Propetro stock hits 52-week high at $12.19
feed.summary: Propetro Holding Corp.'s stock has reached a 52-week high of $12.19, reflecting a 29.84% increase over the past year and a 130.94% surge in six months. The company recently announced an upsized public offering and an amended credit agreement, alongside plans for its PROPWR division to provide power for distributed microgrids in the Permian Basin. Despite appearing overvalued, these developments highlight Propetro's strategic growth and operational efficiency.

feed.title: NVMI Q4 Deep Dive: Market Share Gains and Product Mix Offset Margin Pressures
feed.summary: Nova (NASDAQ:NVMI) reported better-than-expected Q4 CY2025 results, with revenues up 14.3% year-on-year and adjusted EPS beating estimates. Despite a negative market reaction, management highlighted strong demand for advanced semiconductor nodes, strategic customer wins, and growth in its services business, which helped offset margin pressures from product mix shifts. The company projects continued growth in Q1 CY2026, driven by investments in advanced manufacturing and expansion in logic, memory, and packaging markets.

feed.title: What's Going On With Neo-Concept International Stock Friday?
feed.summary: Neo-Concept International Group Holdings Ltd. (NCI) shares rose on Friday after the company announced the closing of a public offering that raised approximately $8.1 million. The company issued 14,850,000 Class A ordinary shares at $0.5454 per share and plans to use the proceeds for business expansion and general working capital. NCI stock is trading significantly above its 20-day and 100-day simple moving averages, indicating strong short-term momentum, with shares up 20.89% at $2.63.

feed.title: Why Is Wells Fargo (WFC) Down 3% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Wells Fargo (WFC) shares have declined by approximately 3% since its last earnings report, underperforming the S&P 500 despite beating Q4 2025 adjusted earnings estimates. The bank's Q4 results were bolstered by higher net interest income and non-interest income but were impacted by a rise in non-performing assets and severance expenses from workforce reductions. Analysts' estimates for WFC have been trending upward, yet the stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) with poor Growth and Momentum scores.

feed.title: Union Pacific (UNP) is a Top Dividend Stock Right Now: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Union Pacific (UNP) is highlighted as a top dividend stock, offering a current dividend yield of 2.11%, which is significantly higher than its industry average and the S&P 500. The company has a history of consistent dividend growth and a manageable payout ratio of 47%, with earnings expected to grow by 7.12% in 2026. This makes UNP an attractive option for income investors, especially given its Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Super Micro Computer's (SMCI) Increased Full year Outlook Still Conservative
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) has seen its stock rise over 10% following an earnings report where the CEO called the company's $40 billion revenue target "conservative." Strong customer demand from data centers and enterprises, particularly in the U.S., contributed significantly to Q2 revenue. Management has increased its full-year 2026 revenue outlook to a minimum of $40 billion, with a strategic focus on diversifying revenue with higher-margin customers like traditional enterprise, cloud, and edge IoT.

feed.title: Citigroup Trades at a Discount to Industry: How to Play the Stock?
feed.summary: Citigroup (C) is trading at a discount compared to its industry and peers like Bank of America and Wells Fargo, despite outperforming them in stock appreciation over the past year. The company is undergoing a strategic transformation, divesting non-core assets, streamlining operations, and investing in wealth management and investment banking, aiming for efficiency and growth. While its discounted valuation, improving earnings, and strong capital position present a compelling case, potential risks from deteriorating asset quality and execution challenges warrant a hold rating for long-term investors.

feed.title: Why Is Bank of America (BAC) Down 0.1% Since Last Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Bank of America (BAC) shares have declined by 0.1% since its last earnings report, despite outperforming the S&P 500. The company's Q4 2025 earnings topped estimates, driven by strong trading numbers and net interest income (NII) growth, though investment banking fees were subdued. Management provided positive guidance and medium-term targets, expecting continued NII growth and improved operating leverage.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share worth $413
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE:TPL), has purchased 1 share of common stock for $413. This transaction increases their direct ownership to 3,479,372 shares. The article also highlights recent strategic developments for Texas Pacific Land, including a partnership with Bolt Data & Energy and a three-for-one stock split, alongside positive analyst ratings from Texas Capital Securities and KeyBanc.

feed.title: Equifax tests former support trendline after steep decline
feed.summary: Equifax (EFX) recently closed at $188.18 after a significant 39% decline from its 2024 highs, placing the stock at a critical juncture. It is now testing a former multi-year ascending support trendline, which has potentially flipped into resistance, a classic technical analysis scenario. The stock is currently trading between this overhead resistance and a major support level at $159.93, with its next move set to determine if this is a bounce opportunity or a continuation of the bearish trend.

feed.title: FactSet: Buy The Drop On This Moat-Worthy Stock (NYSE:FDS)
feed.summary: FactSet Research Systems (FDS) is rated a 'Strong Buy' due to a market overreaction to potential AI disruption, which has pushed its forward P/E to 11.5. The company boasts strong fundamentals, including 95% client retention and 5.9% ASV growth, alongside strategic investments in AI. With management focused on AI integration and robust financial metrics, the stock is poised for double-digit total returns from modest multiple expansion, EPS growth, and a 2.1% dividend yield.

feed.title: Down 19.0% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why Waters (WAT) Looks Ripe for a Turnaround
feed.summary: Waters (WAT) shares have fallen 19% in the last four weeks but appear poised for a turnaround. The stock is in oversold territory with an RSI of 23.31, suggesting potential for a rebound. Additionally, analysts have collectively raised earnings estimates for the current year by 1.1% over the past 30 days, and WAT currently holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), indicating further potential for appreciation.

feed.title: Should Altria Stock Be Part of Your Portfolio Post Q4 Earnings?
feed.summary: Altria Group, Inc. delivered a resilient fourth-quarter 2025 performance, showcasing its business model's durability despite cigarette volume declines and regulatory uncertainties. The company leveraged pricing power, cost management, and strategic smoke-free investments to maintain margins and sustain earnings growth, leading to a 12% stock climb since the earnings release. Despite strong performance, the article suggests a "hold" stance due to modest earnings growth expectations, balancing its discounted valuation and attractive dividend yield against potential upside.

feed.title: AEP contracted large load pipeline doubles to 56 GW
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) announced a significant increase in its contracted large load pipeline, doubling to 56 GW by 2030, largely driven by data centers. The company plans a capital expenditure of $72 billion through 2030, with additional investments expected, and is seeking tariff approvals to ensure new loads pay for necessary infrastructure. AEP also reported an 18% jump in 2025 income due to rate increases and increased retail sales.

feed.title: Cost Pressure or Merchant Solutions Power Play? GPN Heads Into Q4
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. (GPN) is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 results on February 18, 2026, with analysts expecting a year-over-year increase in both earnings and revenues. While the company's Merchant Solutions and Issuer Solutions segments are projected to show growth, increased operating costs are anticipated to partially offset profit expansion. The article also provides a brief overview of the recent performance of other payment industry players like American Express, Mastercard, and Visa.

feed.title: Medtronic Wins FDA Nod For Spine Surgery Tech Designed To Cut Repeat Imaging
feed.summary: Medtronic (NYSE: MDT) has received FDA clearance for its Stealth AXiS surgical system for spine surgery. This new technology aims to provide real-time visualization of anatomical motion during procedures, potentially reducing the need for repeated imaging and streamlining the surgical workflow. While the stock's valuation is seen as a premium, analysts have set an average price target of $110.29, suggesting confidence in its growth prospects.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Roku (NASDAQ:ROKU) Stock
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has increased its price target for Roku (NASDAQ:ROKU) to $137 from $116, maintaining an "overweight" rating and suggesting a potential upside of over 55%. This positive outlook comes after Roku exceeded Q4 earnings and revenue estimates and raised its full-year revenue expectations to $5.5 billion, driven by higher-margin monetization efforts. Despite bullish analyst sentiment and strong Q4 results, concerns remain regarding insider selling, negative net margin, and return on equity, suggesting valuation and profitability are key issues for investors.

feed.title: Tyler Technologies (NYSE:TYL) Price Target Lowered to $335.00 at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has lowered its price target for Tyler Technologies (NYSE:TYL) from $420.00 to $335.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This new target implies a potential upside of 10.56% from the stock's current price, significantly lower than the analyst consensus of a "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $556.29. The article also details recent insider sales and institutional investor activity, alongside the company's financial performance metrics.

feed.title: CVS Pharmacy Is Quietly Becoming Your All?in?One Health Hub — Here’s What Shoppers Are Really Sa
feed.summary: CVS Pharmacy is transforming into a comprehensive health hub, offering prescription services, same-day care, and a wide range of health, wellness, and household essentials. This strategy aims to provide a single, convenient location for routine health needs, leveraging its extensive physical footprint and integrated digital tools. Shoppers appreciate the convenience and rewards but note inconsistent service and price perceptions.
